title: DVD easter eggs
wikitext:
[]: DVD easter eggs             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Aug 04, 2004 2:30 pm An easter egg is basically a hidden extra found in works of fiction or products. ADV's original release of the DVDs contained two, as far as I know, easter eggs. I don't think they work on the perfect collection DVDs. The first one only works on Genesis 0:1. Go to the extras menu, so "Character Bios," is hilighted, press up, then enter, and watch. The other one works on Genesis 0:2. Go to Character Bios, go to the last biography, then press "1" on the DVD remote, a new biography will appear. Edit:Apparently, this one doesn't work, blame it on Frosty555, not me By the way, I didn't discover these, I believe the credit should go to Frosty555 and ShinjisBrother. On a completely unrelated note...

[]:             Quote Post by Crazyman42 » Wed Aug 04, 2004 9:38 pm O_O

[]:             Quote Post by Esion » Wed Aug 04, 2004 9:48 pm I'm not gay, I'm not gay, I just dance in a gay gay way.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Aug 05, 2004 10:42 am I thought you people desereved to see it. [url]http://www.animefromjapan.com[/url] has some DVD easter eggs to several anime, or you could go to [url]http://www.eeggs.com[/url] for general things.

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Thu Aug 05, 2004 4:32 pm Where the HELL did you get that Spider Man thing? I want to scare my friends with it! EDIT: YEAH! 100th post!

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Aug 05, 2004 4:56 pm That would be [url]http://gayspiderman.com[/url].

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Aug 08, 2004 6:20 pm I can testify that, no, the Egg on Collection 0:2, doesn't work on the Perfect Collection DVDs. I wonder why...? Just what is it, anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Sun Aug 08, 2004 6:25 pm The first easter egg unlocks a clean version of the opening/closing themes (no credits). The second easter egg unlocks a hidden bio of the fifth angel.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Aug 08, 2004 6:28 pm The Fifth's bio is not hidden. Presing "1" merely selects the "Back" command. Ramiel's bio is the second to the last, so you're just going back to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sun Aug 08, 2004 6:30 pm Looks like some really mest up with this

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Sun Aug 08, 2004 6:40 pm Hmmm... Oh well, blame Frosty555, not me.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sun Aug 08, 2004 6:41 pm Phaze wrote: Hmmm... Oh well, blame Frosty555, not me.
